t: Does a heart shape have a line of symmetry?
e: Yes! A heart has 1 line of symmetry straight down the middle. The left and right halves match.

t: If you fold a square in half, do both sides match exactly?
o: Only if you fold it a special way
e: Yes! A square has lines of symmetry. Folding it in half (top to bottom, left to right, or corner to corner) gives matching halves.

t: Two triangles can be put together to make a ___.
e: Two identical right triangles can be put together to make a square (or a rectangle or parallelogram, depending on the triangles).

t: What does 'line of symmetry' mean?
o: A line that makes a shape bigger
A line that folds a shape into two matching halves
A line that goes around a shape
A line that makes a shape 3D
e: A line of symmetry divides a shape into two halves that are mirror images -- they match exactly when folded.

t: Does a circle have a line of symmetry?
e: Yes! A circle has many lines of symmetry. Any line through the center divides it into two matching halves.

t: Which shape can you make by putting 2 squares side by side?
e: Two squares side by side make a rectangle that is twice as long as it is tall.

t: Does the letter A have a line of symmetry?
e: The capital letter A has a line of symmetry straight down the middle. The left side mirrors the right side.

t: If you cut a rectangle in half from top to bottom, what 2 shapes do you get?
e: Cutting a rectangle straight down the middle gives you 2 smaller rectangles.

t: Does a square have more than one line of symmetry?
o: Squares have no lines of symmetry
e: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: horizontal, vertical, and 2 diagonal.

t: What shape do you get when you put 4 small triangles together?
o: A bigger triangle or a square
e: Four small triangles can be arranged to make a bigger triangle or a square, depending on how you place them.

t: Does a rectangle have a line of symmetry?
e: Yes! A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry: one horizontal and one vertical through the center.

t: If you fold a shape along a line of symmetry, what happens?
o: The shape turns into a circle
e: When you fold along a line of symmetry, both halves match up perfectly -- one half covers the other exactly.

t: You have 1 big square. You cut it into 4 equal pieces. What shape is each piece?
e: Cutting a square into 4 equal pieces (one horizontal cut, one vertical cut) gives 4 smaller squares.

t: Which of these shapes has NO line of symmetry?
o: Scalene triangle with all different sides
e: A scalene triangle (all sides different, all angles different) has no line of symmetry. The other shapes all have at least one.

t: Two rectangles placed end to end make a ___.
e: Two rectangles placed end to end make one longer rectangle.

t: Does a butterfly have symmetry?
o: Yes, left and right sides match
e: Yes! A butterfly has symmetry. Its left wing mirrors its right wing, with a line of symmetry down the body.

t: If you put 6 triangles together at a point, what shape can you make?
e: Six equilateral triangles arranged around a center point make a hexagon.

t: Does an equilateral triangle (all sides equal) have a line of symmetry?
e: Yes! An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry -- one from each corner to the middle of the opposite side.

t: You split a circle in half. What are the two pieces called?
e: Half of a circle is called a semicircle. 'Semi' means half.

t: Which letter does NOT have a line of symmetry: B, O, X, or F?
e: The letter F has no line of symmetry. B has a horizontal line, O has many, and X has both vertical and horizontal.
